description: Primary-sourced interaction/usability heuristics — Nielsen's 10 + Fitts's/Hick's laws. Re-derived from Nielsen Norman Group originals, not the reference skill's paraphrase.

rules.title: Visibility of system status
rules.rule: The system always keeps users informed of what is going on, through timely feedback. Any action >1s shows progress; state changes are visible.
rules.do: Optimistic UI + spinner past 1s; toast on save.

rules.title: Match between system and the real world
rules.rule: Speak the users' language — words, phrases, concepts familiar to them; follow real-world conventions in a natural, logical order. No internal jargon.

rules.title: User control and freedom
rules.rule: Provide a clearly marked emergency exit — undo and redo. Users pick functions by mistake; let them leave without an extended dialog.
rules.do: Undo on destructive actions; cancel on every modal.

rules.title: Consistency and standards
rules.rule: Follow platform and industry conventions (Jakob's Law — users spend most time on other sites). Same words/actions mean the same thing throughout.

rules.title: Error prevention
rules.rule: Prevent problems before they occur — eliminate error-prone conditions or confirm before commit. Better than good error messages.
rules.do: Disable submit until valid; confirm irreversible deletes.

rules.title: Recognition rather than recall
rules.rule: Minimize memory load — make elements, actions, options visible. Don't force users to remember information across steps.

rules.title: Flexibility and efficiency of use
rules.rule: Accelerators (keyboard shortcuts, defaults, recents) speed up experts without hindering novices; let users tailor frequent actions.

rules.title: Aesthetic and minimalist design
rules.rule: Interfaces contain no irrelevant or rarely-needed information — every extra unit competes with the relevant units and diminishes their visibility.

rules.title: Help users recognize, diagnose, recover from errors
rules.rule: Error messages are in plain language (no codes), precisely state the problem, and constructively suggest a fix.
rules.do: "Email already in use — sign in or use another."

rules.title: Help and documentation
rules.rule: Ideally no docs needed, but when required they are easy to search, focused on the user's task, list concrete steps, and aren't too large.

rules.title: Fitts's Law — size + distance of targets
rules.rule: Time to acquire a target is a function of distance to and size of the target. Make frequent/important targets large and close; use screen edges (infinite depth).
rules.do: Primary CTA large; destructive actions small + far from primary.

rules.title: Hick's Law — choice overload
rules.rule: Decision time grows with the number and complexity of choices. Reduce options, group, use progressive disclosure and sensible defaults.
